items.name: OCP Uniforms
items.whyNeeded: Worn throughout all three phases of Ranger School.
items.selectionTips: Bring enough for the full course without laundry access. Mark everything with your name.

items.name: Jungle Boots
items.whyNeeded: Florida phase involves significant water operations. Non-waterproof boots = trench foot.
items.selectionTips: Break in before the course. Bring two pairs to rotate and dry.

recommendedProducts.name: Bates Jungle Combat Boot
recommendedProducts.reason: Designed specifically for Florida Phase conditions. Drains water fast and dries overnight — critical when you're wet for days at a time.

items.name: Field Notebook (Ranger Handbook)
items.whyNeeded: Essential reference for tactics and procedures used throughout the course.
items.selectionTips: Memorize the key sections before the course. Tab the most-used pages.

recommendedProducts.name: US Army Ranger Handbook SH 21-76
recommendedProducts.reason: The definitive reference for tactics, OPORD formats, and survival used across all three Ranger School phases. Tabs save time when your mind is foggy from sleep deprivation.

items.name: Sleeping Bag (0°F Rated)
items.whyNeeded: Mountain phase temperatures drop below freezing. Hypothermia is a real risk.
items.selectionTips: Mummy bags are most efficient. Ensure it packs small enough to fit in your ruck.

recommendedProducts.name: USGI Modular Sleep System (MSS)
recommendedProducts.reason: The Army-standard system used at Dahlonega. The patrol bag handles 30°F alone; combined with the intermediate bag it's rated to -10°F for Mountain Phase.

items.name: Poncho and Poncho Liner
items.whyNeeded: Primary rain protection and improvised sleeping system.
items.selectionTips: The woobie (poncho liner) is the most versatile piece of kit you'll own.

items.name: Compass (Lensatic)
items.whyNeeded: Land navigation is a core Ranger School skill. A reliable compass is non-negotiable.
items.selectionTips: Practice with it before the course. Know how to shoot an azimuth cold.

recommendedProducts.name: Cammenga Official US Military Tritium Lensatic Compass
recommendedProducts.reason: Standard issue to Army Rangers since the 1990s. Tritium vials glow without batteries for 12+ years — reliable in any condition at Benning or Dahlonega.

items.name: ACE Wrap / First Aid Supplies
items.whyNeeded: Basic self-care for blisters, sprains, and minor injuries.
items.selectionTips: Tape your feet religiously every day to prevent blisters.

items.name: Multi-Tool or Fixed Blade Knife
items.selectionTips: A Leatherman Wave or Signal is ideal. Ensure it meets course regulations.

recommendedProducts.name: Leatherman Wave+ Multi-Tool
recommendedProducts.reason: 25 tools in 8.5 oz. The go-to choice for field repairs, first aid, and survival tasks across all three Ranger School phases.

items.name: Extra Foot Care Supplies
items.selectionTips: Leukotape over moleskin for extreme blister prevention. Apply before blisters form.

recommendedProducts.name: Leukotape P Sports Tape
recommendedProducts.reason: 10× stronger adhesion than standard medical tape. Stays bonded to wet feet through swamp phase — the closest thing to blister-proof available.

items.name: Electrolyte Powder
items.selectionTips: Critical for preventing cramping during high-heat operations.

recommendedProducts.name: Liquid IV Hydration Multiplier
recommendedProducts.reason: Each packet replaces 2–3 bottles of water via cellular transport. Critical for preventing hyponatremia and cramping during Benning Phase heat.

items.name: Ranger Patrol Pack
items.selectionTips: Used for lighter-load patrols. Must be military-colored.

items.name: Unauthorized Food / Supplements
items.description: Anything not in the standard issue ration
items.notes: Contraband food can result in recycle or dismissal. Follow course rules exactly.
